heading: Reserve Fund
label: 4017a Reserve Fund

children.text: the payment of claims;

children.label: B

children.label: C
children.text: the repayment of amounts outstanding under any note or other obligation issued by the Administrator under .section 4016(a) of this title
1 percent of the sum of the total potential loss exposure of all outstanding flood insurance policies in force in the prior fiscal year; or

such higher percentage as the Administrator determines to be appropriate, taking into consideration any circumstance that may raise a significant risk of substantial future losses to the Reserve Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: to maintain the reserve ratio required under subsection (b); and

children.label: B
children.text: to achieve such reserve ratio, if the actual balance of such reserve is below the amount required under subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: the expected operating expenses of the Reserve Fund;

children.label: B
children.text: the insurance loss expenditures under the flood insurance program;

children.label: C
children.text: any investment income generated under the flood insurance program; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other factor that the Administrator determines appropriate.

children.heading: Rates
children.label: A Rates
children.text: In exercising the authority granted under paragraph (1), the Administrator shall be subject to all other provisions of this chapter, including any provisions relating to chargeable premium rates or annual increases of such rates.

children.heading: Use of additional annual insurance premiums
children.label: B Use of additional annual insurance premiums
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law or any agreement entered into by the Administrator, the Administrator shall ensure that all amounts attributable to the establishment or increase of annual insurance premiums under paragraph (1) are transferred to the Administrator for deposit into the Reserve Fund, to be available for meeting the expected future obligations of the flood insurance program as described in subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Deposit of premium surcharges
children.label: 4 Deposit of premium surcharges
children.text: The Administrator shall deposit in the Reserve Fund any surcharges collected pursuant to .section 4015a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Beginning in fiscal year 2013 and not ending until the fiscal year in which the ratio required under subsection (b) is achieved, in each such fiscal year the Administrator shall place in the Reserve Fund an amount equal to not less than 7.5 percent of the reserve ratio required under subsection (b).

children.heading: Amount satisfied
children.label: 2 Amount satisfied
children.text: As soon as the ratio required under subsection (b) is achieved, and except as provided in paragraph (3), the Administrator shall not be required to set aside any amounts for the Reserve Fund.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 3 Exception
children.text: If at any time after the ratio required under subsection (b) is achieved, the Reserve Fund falls below the required ratio under subsection (b), the Administrator shall place in the Reserve Fund for that fiscal year an amount equal to not less than 7.5 percent of the reserve ratio required under subsection (b).
describes and details the specific concerns of the Administrator regarding the consequences of the reserve ratio not being achieved;
demonstrates how such consequences would harm the long-term financial soundness of the flood insurance program; and
indicates the maximum attainable reserve ratio for that particular fiscal year.

children.heading: Investment
children.label: f Investment
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall invest such amounts of the Reserve Fund as the Secretary determines advisable in obligations issued or guaranteed by the United States.
